text.title: Service Ownership Map Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every service-ownership map must satisfy so triage tools can route alerts to a named human without ambiguity.

rule.statement: The map MUST be a single checked-in file (YAML or JSON) under version control; no wiki pages, no spreadsheets, no Notion docs as authoritative.
rule.rationale: Triage routing needs a service→owner map; if the artefact lives in tribal knowledge it goes stale within a quarter. Version control gives diff history, code review, and machine-readable parsing for the alerting tool.

example.code: # services.yaml — checked in at ops/services.yaml services: checkout-api: primary_owner: "@alex.kim" escalation: "@team-payments" slack: "#payments-alerts" runbook: "https://wiki/runbooks/checkout-api"

rule.statement: Each service entry MUST name a single primary_owner (handle of one human) plus exactly one escalation handle (team or named secondary). No bare "team" without a handle.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership. P6 teams repeatedly rediscover that "@platform" channels go unanswered at 02:00 unless one human owns the page.

rule.statement: The map MUST be refreshed on every service create/rename/retire and reviewed quarterly; entries with last_reviewed >90 days fail CI.
rule.rationale: Ownership maps decay fastest of any infra artefact (people leave, teams reorg). Forcing a CI failure converts decay from invisible to visible.

rule.statement: The alerting tool MUST consume the map programmatically (via Sentry "owners" file, Datadog tag, or webhook lookup) — never via copy-paste into the tool UI.
rule.rationale: Manual sync between two sources guarantees drift. A single ingestion path keeps the file authoritative.

example.description: Engineer edits Datadog monitor "notify" field directly in the UI while services.yaml says someone else.
example.reason: Two sources of truth → triage routes to the stale UI value; the on-call owner in YAML never learns the alert fired.

rule.statement: Every entry MUST link to a runbook URL that exists and renders; broken runbook links fail CI.
rule.rationale: A named owner with no runbook still wastes triage time at 02:00. Pairing the two converts "who do I page" into "who do I page AND what do they do first".
